evaluations.title: Percolation Threshold Theory x T Cell Infiltration in Solid Tumors
concerns: Ashworth 2015 already applied percolation theory to cell invasion in collagen scaffolds — conceptual bridge partially exists
Active T cell motility complicates passive percolation predictions — critical exponents may differ from standard
ECM dynamic remodeling timescale must be explicitly addressed
key_prior_art: Ashworth et al. 2015 Advanced Healthcare Materials — percolation theory for cell invasion in collagen scaffolds
Nicolas-Boluda et al. 2021 eLife — LOX inhibition + anti-PD-1 improving T cell migration
evaluations.novelty_assessment: Percolation framework for collagen cell migration exists (Ashworth 2015), but tumor immunology application with LOX as control parameter and critical exponent universality predictions is genuinely novel

evaluations.title: Acoustic Filter-Bank Theory x Plant Bioacoustics
concerns: CRITICAL: Core bridge concept (trichomes as acoustic antennae with filter-bank frequency selectivity) is PUBLISHED — Liu et al. 2017 Biophysical Journal, Liu et al. 2021 Extreme Mechanics Letters
The 2021 paper explicitly models trichome ensembles as frequency-selective filters with band gaps — this IS the proposed bridge
MSL/MCA channel coupling to trichome resonance is the only genuinely novel element
Frequency mismatch between trichome fundamental resonance (~8 kHz) and plant ultrasonic emissions (20-100 kHz)
key_prior_art: Liu et al. 2017 Biophysical Journal — Arabidopsis Leaf Trichomes as Acoustic Antennae
Liu et al. 2021 Extreme Mechanics Letters — Trichome ensembles selectively vibrate in herbivore frequency range with band gaps
Liu et al. 2022 Biophysical Journal — Acoustic radiation force on tomato trichomes
required_modifications: Must acknowledge Liu 2017/2021 as prior art and build beyond them
Shift novelty claim to MSL/MCA channel-resonator coupling mechanism
Address frequency mismatch: trichome fundamental ~8 kHz vs plant emissions 20-100 kHz
Consider replacing if primary bridge is entirely prior art
evaluations.novelty_assessment: Primary bridge (trichome filter-bank) is prior art. Only the MSL/MCA channel coupling is genuinely novel.

evaluations.title: Classical Nucleation Theory x Ferroptosis Labile Iron Pool Dynamics
concerns: 2025 bioRxiv paper: LIP dynamics do NOT drive ferroptosis potentiation in colorectal cancer cells — LIP did not measurably increase during ferroptosis induction
CNT may not apply to sub-6nm ferritin core dissolution — classical thermodynamic quantities questionable at nanoscale
3rd ferroptosis session (domain saturation after S005, S006)
Surface energy gamma of ferrihydrite in intracellular dissolution context is poorly characterized
key_prior_art: 2025 bioRxiv — LIP dynamics do not drive ferroptosis potentiation in CRC cells
Ferritin nucleation literature (JACS 2025) — iron core formation via L-chain nucleation sites (reverse direction from proposed bridge)
required_modifications: Must address 2025 paper showing LIP dynamics decoupled from ferroptosis potentiation
Must justify CNT at sub-6nm scale or use alternative formalism
Should include non-ferroptosis applications to avoid domain saturation
evaluations.novelty_assessment: Dissolution-as-reverse-nucleation framing is novel. But structural concerns about CNT applicability and LIP-ferroptosis coupling are significant.

evaluations.title: Linear Viscoelastic Creep Theory x Biofilm Antibiotic Tolerance
concerns: Biofilm rheology field is more active than Scout's disjointness claim suggests (Stoodley 1999, Peterson 2015, 2023 Bayesian estimation papers)
Multiple mechanisms of biofilm tolerance beyond EPS barrier (persisters, dormancy, efflux) limit explanatory scope
Partial overlap with S011 (Cartilage biphasic x Biofilm mechanics) — same Field C domain
key_prior_art: Peterson et al. 2015 FEMS Microbiol Rev — viscoelasticity and recalcitrance to mechanical/chemical challenges
Frontiers 2023 — perspective on viscoelastic properties and immune cell interactions
2025 npj Biofilms — biofilm structure as key factor in antibiotic tolerance
evaluations.novelty_assessment: Biofilm rheology is studied, but the specific creep-compliance-to-MIC quantitative mapping and G'/G" crossover frequency as tolerance predictor is genuinely novel. Failed paradigm recycling logic is sound and well-reasoned.

evaluations.title: Griffith Fracture Mechanics x Bacterial Cell Wall Failure Under Beta-Lactam Stress
concerns: CRITICAL: Fracture mechanics already applied to bacterial cell walls — Zhou et al. 2015 Science (crack propagation in S. aureus), Lam et al. 2018 (fracture mechanics FEA model), Wong et al. 2019 (mechanics of lysis)
PG mesh spacing (~2 nm) too small for continuum Griffith — must use lattice fracture models
Dynamic PG remodeling (continuous synthesis + autolysis) complicates static fracture analysis
Scout's claim that existing models predict only uniform thinning is partially incorrect
key_prior_art: Zhou et al. 2015 Science — crack propagation in S. aureus cell wall (fracture mechanics applied to bacteria)
Lam et al. 2018 Biomech Model Mechanobiol — fracture mechanics FEA modeling of cell separation
Wong et al. 2019 Biophysical Journal — mechanics and dynamics of bacterial cell lysis with critical defect size
required_modifications: Must cite Zhou 2015, Lam 2018, Wong 2019 as prior art applying fracture mechanics to bacterial walls
Must use lattice fracture models instead of continuum Griffith given ~2 nm mesh spacing
Must address dynamic PG remodeling during beta-lactam exposure
Novelty must focus on beta-lactam-specific defect patterns + extreme value lysis statistics
evaluations.novelty_assessment: Fracture mechanics in bacterial cell walls exists. Novel elements: beta-lactam defect-specific application, stress corrosion cracking via autolysins, extreme value statistics for lysis timing.

evaluations.title: Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy x Gut Microbiome Metabolic State
concerns: CRITICAL: Disjointness claim is FALSE — Moysidou 2024 uses EIS to monitor gut microbiome in 3D bioelectronic platform
Multiple groups (MIT, Cambridge, UCSD) actively developing ingestible electrochemical gut sensors
Rezaei & Bhatt 2025 Nature Electronics: miniaturized ingestible redox sensor for gut
Inda-Webb 2023 Nature Comms: self-powered ingestible biosensing for GI metabolites
Known unsolved engineering challenges: biofouling, reference electrode stability, signal attribution in complex gut environment
Metabolic fingerprint specificity not demonstrated in mixed-culture environments
key_prior_art: Moysidou et al. 2024 Small Science — EIS monitoring of gut microbiome in 3D bioelectronic platform
Rezaei & Bhatt 2025 Nature Electronics — ingestible redox sensor for gut
Inda-Webb et al. 2023 Nature Communications — self-powered ingestible GI metabolite sensor
2023 MDPI Biosensors review — EIS-based biofilm sensing
required_modifications: Reclassify as PARTIALLY_EXPLORED — disjointness claim is demonstrably false
Position hypotheses relative to existing ingestible sensor literature
Scope down from general metabolic fingerprint to specific testable predictions
Address biofouling, reference drift, and signal attribution explicitly
evaluations.novelty_assessment: The intersection is well-established with multiple published papers and active research groups. Not disjoint.

best_target.rationale: Highest composite and impact. Ashworth 2015 precedent is a concern but tumor immunology application with LOX control is genuinely novel.

second_best.rationale: Failed paradigm recycling logic is sound. G'/G" crossover to MIC prediction is novel. Exploration slot value for untested strategy.

weakest_target.rationale: Disjointness claim demonstrably false. Active research area with published solutions.

summary.pipeline_recommendation: PROCEED — T1 and T4 are both suitable for full pipeline investment
